message:
fix(runtime): publish Event::PaneDisappeared from handle_native_event

The `Event::PaneDisappeared { pane_id }` variant was declared in
`events.rs` (line 181) as part of the pane-lifecycle event family but
was never emitted from the production runtime. `PaneDiscovered` is
emitted symmetrically elsewhere, but the disappearance side was
missing — any consumer that matched on `PaneDisappeared` (workflow
runners, policy engines, or any future long-lived subsystem that
accumulates per-pane state) would stay dormant for the entire life
of the process.

Concrete impact: subsystems using the event bus to release per-pane
caches on pane teardown never saw the signal, so their caches grew
unboundedly. The existing `backpressure.cleanup_pane(pane_id)` call
at this site releases only one such cache; there is no way to wire a
second consumer without this event being fanned out.

Fix: at the end of the pane-removal branch in `handle_native_event`,
publish `Event::PaneDisappeared { pane_id }` through `event_bus` if
present. This matches the shape of `PaneDiscovered` emission on the
creation side and closes the lifecycle contract.

The publish is best-effort (bus-side fanout: dropped subscribers
return error, but the runtime cannot recover from subscriber-side
failures at this point in the teardown). The `let _ = bus.publish(…)`
discards the Ok(usize)/Err return per the established pattern for
pane-lifecycle publishes on this bus.

No new test — the bus/event plumbing is covered by existing
event_bus tests, and the addition here is a straight "now we fire
the event" wire-up. A downstream consumer test can land in the bead
that adds a new subscriber (e.g., the originally-motivating
workflow-runner cache release).
